metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.description: Use when you need to add or review Mongock MongoDB data migrations in a Quarkus application — including the Quarkiverse Mongock extension, Quarkus MongoDB client configuration, migrate-at-start, @ChangeUnit classes, lock/transaction settings, and Quarkus test verification. This should trigger for requests such as Add Mongock migrations in Quarkus; Configure quarkus-mongock; Review Quarkus MongoDB data migrations; Create Mongock change units for Quarkus MongoDB; Review Mongock migration ordering in a Quarkus service.

prompt.title: Quarkus - MongoDB migrations (Mongock)
prompt.goal:
Apply Mongock migration guidelines for Quarkus and Quarkus MongoDB client/Panache Mongo.
**What is covered in this Skill?**
- Quarkiverse `quarkus-mongock` extension and Quarkus MongoDB client setup - `quarkus.mongock.*` configuration, including `migrate-at-start` - Code-first `@ChangeUnit` migrations with `@Execution` and rollback hooks - Locking, idempotency, transaction limits, and default-client constraints - Quarkus tests with Dev Services or Testcontainers MongoDB - Coordination with `@415-frameworks-quarkus-mongodb`
**Scope:** Apply recommendations based on the reference rules and good/bad examples.

constraints.constraints-description: Compile before Mongock or MongoDB migration changes; verify after changes.

constraint-list.constraint: **MANDATORY**: Run `./mvnw compile` or `mvn compile` before applying any change
**VERIFY**: Run `./mvnw clean verify` or `mvn clean verify` after applying improvements
**BEFORE APPLYING**: Read the reference for detailed rules and good/bad patterns

**EXTENSION COMPATIBILITY**: Before adding `quarkus-mongock`, verify the latest Quarkiverse Mongock release and Quarkus extension catalog compatibility with the active Quarkus platform. If the latest extension targets a significantly older Quarkus version, stop and document the incompatibility instead of applying changes.
**EDGE CASE**: If the application uses multiple MongoDB clients, verify Quarkiverse Mongock support before applying a default-client migration design

trigger-list.trigger: Create Mongock change units for Quarkus MongoDB
Review Mongock migration ordering in a Quarkus service

step.step-title: Read references and inspect MongoDB setup
step.step-content: Read `references/416-frameworks-quarkus-mongodb-migrations-mongock.md`, `references/416-frameworks-quarkus-mongodb-migrations-mongock-antipatterns.md`, and `references/416-frameworks-quarkus-mongodb-migrations-mongock-parallel-change.md`, then inspect `pom.xml`, Quarkus MongoDB configuration, and existing `@415-frameworks-quarkus-mongodb` persistence patterns.

step.step-title: Verify extension compatibility and execution policy
step.step-content: Verify the Quarkiverse `quarkus-mongock` release and Quarkus extension catalog compatibility before configuring MongoDB connection settings, migration scan behavior, and startup vs explicit `MongockFactory` execution.
Implement/refactor `@ChangeUnit` classes with idempotent operations, explicit ordering, rollback hooks, and safe lock/transaction settings.
Execute build/tests, including Quarkus tests with Dev Services or Testcontainers MongoDB where feasible, and summarize outcomes and follow-up risks.
